---
title: "Workshop Exercise: Emissions Data - Table, Map, and Chart"

format:
  html:
    toc: true

output-file: index.html

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Introduction

In this exercise, you will work with a CO2 emissions dataset downloaded from Gapminder and produce a report with three tabs: a data table, a line chart, and a choropleth map.

The goal is to roughly replicate the [Our World in Data visualization page on consumption-based CO<sub>2</sub> emissions](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/consumption-co2-emissions?tab=table&time=2000..2022).

Be sure to view that page to get an idea of the final product.

# Setup

- You should have forked and cloned this repository to your local machine.
- Create and select a virtual environment in VS Code.
- Install the following packages:
  - pandas
  - plotly
  - itables
  - ipykernel
  - jupyter
- Download the Gapminder `CO2 Total emissions` dataset and save it inside a folder named `data`.

# Data Import

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from itables import show
```

```{python}
# Load the data using a relative path
emissions = pd.read_csv("data/co2_cons.csv")
```

# Table Section

Our goal is to create a table showing emissions for selected years and calculate absolute and relative changes.

```{python}
# Subset the data to include name, 2000, and 2022
table_df = emissions[["name", "2000", "2022"]].copy()

# Calculate absolute change
table_df["Absolute Change"] = (
    table_df["2022"] - table_df["2000"]
)

# Calculate relative change
table_df["Relative Change"] = (
    table_df["Absolute Change"] / table_df["2000"]
) * 100

# Round to 0 decimal places and add a percentage sign
table_df["Relative Change"] = (
    table_df["Relative Change"]
    .round(0)
    .astype(str)
    + "%"
)
```

# Chart Section

Our goal is to create a line chart from 1990 to 2022 for five selected countries.

```{python}
# Reshape the dataset from wide to long format
emissions_long = emissions.melt(
    id_vars=["geo", "name"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="emissions",
)

# Convert year to numeric
emissions_long["year"] = pd.to_numeric(
    emissions_long["year"],
    errors="coerce",
)

# Convert emissions to numeric and replace the unicode minus sign
emissions_long["emissions"] = pd.to_numeric(
    emissions_long["emissions"]
    .astype(str)
    .str.replace("−", "-"),
    errors="coerce",
)

# Keep years from 1990 to 2022
emissions_long_1990_2022 = emissions_long.query(
    "1990 <= year <= 2022"
).copy()

# Select five countries
emissions_long_subset = emissions_long_1990_2022.query(
    "name in ['Germany', 'France', 'Italy', 'Spain', 'United Kingdom']"
)

# Create the line chart
fig_chart = px.line(
    emissions_long_subset,
    x="year",
    y="emissions",
    color="name",
    title="CO₂ Emissions (1990–2022)",
    labels={
        "year": "Year",
        "emissions": "CO₂ emissions",
        "name": "Country",
    },
)
```

# Mapping Section

The goal is to create an animated choropleth map showing global emissions from 1990 to 2022.

```{python}
# Convert the existing ISO3 codes to uppercase
emissions_long_1990_2022["country_code"] = (
    emissions_long_1990_2022["geo"].str.upper()
)

# Create the animated choropleth map
fig_map = px.choropleth(
    emissions_long_1990_2022,
    locations="country_code",
    color="emissions",
    hover_name="name",
    animation_frame="year",
    title="Global CO₂ Emissions (1990–2022)",
    labels={
        "emissions": "CO₂ emissions",
        "year": "Year",
    },
)
```

# Results

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Table

```{python}
show(table_df)
```

## Chart

```{python}
fig_chart.show()
```

## Map

```{python}
fig_map.show()
```

:::

# Deploying to GitHub Pages

As a final step, follow the steps outlined in the prework to deploy the report to GitHub Pages. You will be asked to share a link to the report in the course portal.
